competencies.name: Research Design & Methodology
competencies.description: Selects the right method for the research question; designs studies with appropriate controls; distinguishes generative from evaluative research; scopes studies to the decision being made.
positive_indicators: matches method to research question and decision type
distinguishes generative, evaluative, and validating research
scopes the study to what the team actually needs to decide
anticipates threats to study validity
discusses sample size and recruitment criteria with rationale
negative_indicators: defaults to one method regardless of the research question
cannot explain when to use qualitative vs. quantitative methods
designs a study that cannot answer the stated question
ignores constraints like timeline or budget in study design

competencies.name: Participant Facilitation
competencies.description: Conducts interviews and usability tests without leading participants; builds rapport quickly; probes for depth; manages time effectively during sessions.
positive_indicators: uses open-ended questions and avoids leading questions
probes for underlying motivation, not just stated preference
builds rapport quickly while maintaining research objectivity
manages time across a session without cutting off participants
negative_indicators: asks leading or yes/no questions
accepts surface-level answers without probing
rushes participants or over-talks during sessions
cannot describe how to recover a session going off-track

competencies.name: Analysis & Synthesis
competencies.description: Turns raw data into actionable insights; identifies patterns across participants; distinguishes signal from noise; avoids over-claiming from qualitative data.
positive_indicators: describes a systematic process for analyzing qualitative data
identifies themes across participants rather than quoting one
distinguishes outlier observations from patterns
separates what participants said from what they meant
anchors insights to evidence, not interpretation alone
negative_indicators: quotes a single participant as representative
cannot describe how themes were derived from data
conflates observation with interpretation
over-generalizes from small or unrepresentative samples

competencies.name: Insight Communication & Storytelling
competencies.description: Structures findings to drive decisions; tailors delivery to audience; uses evidence appropriately; connects insights to product or business implications.
positive_indicators: leads with the implication, not the methodology
tailors detail level to the audience (PM vs. engineer vs. executive)
uses concrete user quotes and examples to ground findings
connects insights explicitly to the decision the team must make
negative_indicators: buries the finding in methodology details
delivers the same presentation regardless of audience
presents findings without connecting to a product decision
cannot describe how findings should change the team's direction

competencies.name: Stakeholder Influence
competencies.description: Builds research credibility with PMs, designers, and engineers; pushes back when research is misused; advocates for the user while being pragmatic about constraints.
positive_indicators: describes how they earn trust from teams that are skeptical of research
pushes back when a stakeholder misinterprets a finding
advocates for user needs while acknowledging business constraints
involves stakeholders in the research process, not just the readout
negative_indicators: accepts misinterpretation of findings without correction
cannot describe how to build credibility with a skeptical team
presents research in isolation from the decision context
does not adapt communication based on stakeholder feedback

competencies.name: Ethical Research Practices
competencies.description: Obtains informed consent; protects participant privacy and data; considers equity and inclusion in participant recruitment; reports findings honestly without omitting inconvenient results.
positive_indicators: describes consent process and how they explain data use
actively recruits for diversity and explains why it matters
reports negative or inconclusive findings honestly
discusses data retention and anonymization practices
negative_indicators: cannot describe how they obtain and document informed consent
recruits only convenient participants without questioning the bias
omits inconclusive findings from reports
has no practice around participant data privacy
